intro: Aphanactis obtusata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Aphanactis obtusata include sesquiterpene lactones, such as 11beta-hydroxy-eudesmanolide and 11beta-hydroxy-eudesmone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aphanactis obtusata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and essential oils.
